REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR HEARING ON VERIFIED PETITION FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDAMUS; PETITIONER'S WRIT IS DENIED. RESPONDENT CORRECTLY INTERPRETED GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 65915. PETITIONER'S PROJECT IS NOT ELIGIBLE UNDER THE STATUTE BECAUSE THE LEGALIZATION OF UNLAWFUL DWELLING UNITS DOES NOT CONSTITUTE A "HOUSING DEVELOPMENT." THE STATUTE'S PLAIN TEXT SUPPORTS RESPONDENT'S ARGUMENT THAT A "HOUSING DEVELOPMENT" REQUIRES CONSTRUCTION OF NEW UNITS. FOR INSTANCE, SECTION 65915(B)(1) MAKES CLEAR THAT AN APPLICANT MUST "CONSTRUCT A HOUSING DEVELOPMENT" TO RECEIVE THE DENSITY BONUS LAW'S BENEFITS. PETITIONER HAS ALSO NOT SHOWN THAT THE UNITS ARE SUBDIVISIONS OR COMMON INTEREST DEVELOPMENTS AS DEFINED BY CIVIL CODE SECTION 4100, NOR THAT THERE WOULD BE A NET INCREASE IN AVAILABLE UNITS. PETITIONER ALSO CITES NO CASES WHERE PLANS TO LEGALIZE UNITS WERE ELIGIBLE UNDER THE STATUTE. BECAUSE THE PROJECT IS NOT ELIGIBLE UNDER THE STATUTE, THE REMAINING DETERMINATIONS BELOW REGARDING HOW TO CALCULATE THE UNITS ARE MOOT.ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: VICTOR M. HWANG ; CLERK: L. YOUNG ; REPORTED: BRIANNA RUDD; CSR# 13668; BRIANNARUDD@GMAIL.COM; 209-747-1306 (D501)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Hearing On Verified Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus
Off Calendar May-27-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Emily Brough; 415-956-8100; Zacks & Freedman; appearing as counsel for petitioner.
Brian Brossman; 415-554-4690; Deputy City Attorney; appearing as counsel for respondent.
Reporter: Brianna Rudd; CSR# 13668; briannarudd@gmail.com; 209-747-1306

Petitioner contests the courts tentative ruling. Argued. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER'S MOTION FOR HEARING ON VERIFIED PETITION FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDAMUS

Petitioner's Writ is DENIED. Respondent correctly interpreted Government Code Section 65915. Petitioner's project is not eligible under the statute because the legalization of unlawful dwelling units does not constitute a "housing development." The statute's plain text supports Respondent's argument that a "housing development" requires construction of new units. For instance, Section 65915(b)(1) makes clear that an applicant must "construct a housing development" to receive the Density Bonus Law's benefits. Petitioner has also not shown that the units are subdivisions or common interest developments as defined by Civil Code Section 4100, nor that there would be a net increase in available units. Petitioner also cites no cases where plans to legalize units were eligible under the statute. Because the project is not eligible under the statute, the remaining determinations below regarding how to calculate the units are moot.

Order signed. Judge: Victor M. Hwang ; Clerk: L. Young ; Reported: Brianna Rudd; CSR# 13668; briannarudd@gmail.com; 209-747-1306 (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Hearing On Verified Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus
